title: dryopteris aemula subsp. liliana
intro: Dryopteris aemula subsp. liliana, also known as Lilian's wood fern, is a subspecies of the Dryopteris aemula species, a type of fern native to Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Lilian's wood fern include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory health, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and act as an antimicrobial agent. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or using them in tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
